'''Ryan Atkins''' (born 7 October 1985) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|ce}} for [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and formerly [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the top flight. Atkins joined the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on a permanent deal and later joined the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the Super League.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Atkins was born in [[Pudsey]], West Yorkshire, England.
He played rugby league for his local team [[Stanningley]] and rugby union for Bramley Phoenix.

==Playing career==
===Bradford===
Atkins began his career with [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] but failed to make a first-team appearance before joining [[Wakefield Trinity]] midway through 2006's [[Super League XI]].

===Warrington===
He signed for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on a three-year deal in September 2009, following [[Richie Myler]] from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].

Atkins played for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6-30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=2010-08-28|access-date=2010-08-28}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628|title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012!|date=25 August 2012|work=Warrington Wolves Official Site|access-date=28 August 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628|archive-date=17 December 2013}}</ref>

Atkins played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Atkins played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>
Atkins played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>
Atkins played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>
Atkins played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===York City Knights===
On 15 September 2020, it was announced that Atkins would join [[York City Knights]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|date=15 September 2020|access-date=5 December 2023|website=www.rugby-league.com|title=Ryan Atkins set for York switch in 2021|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/23710/ryan-atkins-set-for-york-switch-in-2021}}</ref>

On 13 November 2020 the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] announced a three-month ban for Atkins for placing a bet on former club [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|date=13 November 2020|access-date=26 December 2020|website=BBC Sport|title=Ryan Atkins: York-bound centre banned for three months after bet on Wakefield game|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54935741}}</ref> Atkins retired at the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Atkins to retire |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/35990/ryan-atkins-to-retire |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=9 September 2021}}</ref>

==International career==
His impressive club form earned him a call-up and was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=2008-09-29|access-date=2008-10-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=2008-10-07}}</ref>
